chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D-3497 of 2023 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1.For hearing of CMA No.16309/2022 2.For hearing of main case. 17.03.2025 Mr.Umair Bachani, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr.Sandeep Malani, A.A.G. Mr.Zulfiqar Ali, Focal Person, Agriculture Department, Government of Sindh is present. On the last date following order was passed:- “10.03.2025 Mr.Umair Bachani, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr.Sandeep Malani, A.A.G. Per learned A.A.G. there is absolutely no entitlement of the petitioners to the relief claimed as demonstrated from the Annexures to the comments filed by the Department. Learned counsel for the petitioners seeks time to obtain instructions. At his request adjourned to 17.03.2025.” Today, learned counsel for the petitioners remains unable to demonstrate any subsisting entitlement of the petitioners to claim regularization, even otherwise, the matter of regularization is a settled law as seen in the cases decided by Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in Vice Chancellor Agriculture University Peshawar & Others vs. Muhammad Shafiq & Others reported as 2024 SCMR 527, Full bench of this Court in Muhammad Arif vs. Federation of Pakistan reported as 2025 P.L.C. (C.S) 93 and Division Bench of this Court in Mumtaz Ali Panhwar & Others vs. Province of Sindh & Others reported as 2024 P.L.C. (C.S) 1487. In mutatis mutandis application of the ratio illumined in the aforesaid judgments, this petition is found to be misconceived, hence, dismissed.
